hooks.command: if [ -f package.json ] && grep -q '"test"' package.json; then npm test 2>&1 | tail -20; elif [ -f Makefile ] && grep -q '^test:' Makefile; then make test 2>&1 | tail -20; elif [ -f pyproject.toml ] || [ -f setup.py ]; then python -m pytest --tb=short 2>&1 | tail -20; else echo 'No test runner detected — skipping'; fi

FILE=$(python3 -c "import json,sys; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print(d.get('tool_input',{}).get('file_path',''))" 2>/dev/null); if [ -z "$FILE" ] || [ ! -f "$FILE" ]; then exit 0; fi; EXT="${FILE##*.}"; case "$EXT" in js|jsx|ts|tsx) npx eslint --fix "$FILE" 2>/dev/null || true ;; py) python -m ruff check --fix "$FILE" 2>/dev/null || python -m flake8 "$FILE" 2>/dev/null || true ;; esac

hooks.prompt: The agent is about to run a bash command. Check if it is destructive (rm -rf on important dirs, DROP TABLE/DATABASE, formatting disks, overwriting critical files, pushing to main without PR). Respond with JSON: {"decision": "block", "reason": "..."} if destructive, or {"decision": "approve", "reason": "..."} if safe.

A subagent just finished its task. Check if its output is complete: does it have a clear conclusion, actionable items or findings, and proper formatting? Respond with JSON: {"decision": "block", "reason": "..."} if incomplete or vague, or {"decision": "approve", "reason": "..."} if complete.

hooks.command: echo '🔔 Claude needs your attention' && if command -v notify-send &>/dev/null; then notify-send 'Claude Code' 'Waiting for your validation'; elif command -v osascript &>/dev/null; then osascript -e 'display notification "Waiting for your validation" with title "Claude Code"'; fi
